title: Blue cheese chicken – oh so tasty
excerpt: Look, my children aren't fond of blue cheese, but blue cheese chicken I make on request. They practically hang off the chicken. Then if I want to make guests feel special, I make it too. There's just something grand about blue cheese – probably because I didn't know it as a child …

seo.title: Blue cheese chicken - oh so tasty - Marinda Kook

html:
Look, my children aren't fond of blue cheese, but blue cheese chicken I make on request. They practically hang off the chicken. Then if I want to make guests feel special, I make it too. There's just something grand about blue cheese – probably because I didn't know it as a child.

  1. 6 chicken fillets (without skin or bones)
  2. 500 ml flour (I know it's too much, but I don't want to struggle)
  3. 4 eggs lightly beaten – just with a fork
  4. 200g breadcrumbs (the kind you buy in the shop is usually where the spices are)
  5. 250ml cream
  6. 1 wedge of blue cheese

NOW:

  • set up three plates/bowls in a row. In one pour the flour, then the eggs and then the breadcrumbs
  • press the chicken first into the flour, then the eggs and then into the breadcrumbs
  • pack it into an ovenproof dish
  • pour the cream over
  • crumble the blue cheese over
  • bake it at 190 degrees Celsius covered with foil for 20 minutes
  • remove the foil and bake it uncovered for another 20 minutes

It's a real treat. I served mine with couscous and chilli – and of course a glass of wine.
